signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.pair_trade_rationale: Rates and fiscal repricing usually favor self-funded quality over financing-dependent growth exposures.
signal.value_chain_notes: This is a macro/rates regime signal and should only map into sector chains when a more specific bottleneck is visible.

notes: Euro zone inflation hit 2.5% in March (ECB data confirmed via CNBC, March 31), smashing through the 2% target as energy costs soar. French CPI MoM jumped to 0.9% from 0.6% previous. Goldman Sachs rotating portfolios for stagflation risk. The hot French CPI and ECB hawkish commentary (Muller April hike + broader ECB inflation fight) support front_end_yield_move. EUR strength consistent with partial usd_trend_shift (USD weaker vs EUR as inflation differential widens). Goldman Sachs confirming stagflation risk partially validates the usd_trend_shift direction, though the causal chain is more about EUR strength than USD weakness specifically.

signal.title: ECB's Muller: Can't rule out rate hike in April
signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: March 2022 rate hikes crushed tech (down 30% that year). Rate hikes typically pressure growth stocks and rate-sensitive sectors. Dollar strengthens on yield differential. Bond yields climb.
signal.pair_trade_rationale: Rates and fiscal repricing usually favor self-funded quality over financing-dependent growth exposures.
signal.value_chain_notes: This is a macro/rates regime signal and should only map into sector chains when a more specific bottleneck is visible.

notes: ECB's Muller explicitly stating a rate hike cannot be ruled out in April is a strongly hawkish signal consistent with the Eurozone CPI hitting 2.5% (well above 2% target) and the ECB's ongoing inflation fight. Combined with Lagarde challenging Bessent on Iran war fallout at G7, ECB hawkish credentials are confirmed. front_end_yield_move confirmed via EU bond yields rising. usd_trend_shift confirmed via EUR strength as inflation differential vs USD widens.

signal.title: Private lenders delay reckoning with payment concessions on stressed debt
signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: Jan 2023 JPM miss triggered regional bank selloff. Big bank misses often spark contagion fears — investors worry if JPM is struggling, regional banks are in trouble. Credit spreads widen.

notes: Reuters confirmed (March 31): Private credit lenders working with borrowers to delay cash payments and extend concessions on stressed debt — a clear sign of credit stress. Warren Buffett also warning of fragility in the banking system from increased connection to non-bank players (Bloomberg, March 31). Pattern p018 (Regional Banking Crisis 2023) analog: credit stress triggers financials volatility and safe-haven flows into Treasuries (treasury_bid). financials_volatility confirmed via Buffett warnings + credit market stress signals. treasury_bid confirmed by US Bonds Rally on March 31 (Bloomberg Markets: 'US Bonds Rally as Traders Bet on Fed Rate Cut in About-Face') — flight to safety amid financial sector stress.
verification_note: Reuters confirmed (March 31): Private credit lenders working with borrowers to delay cash payments and extend concessions on stressed debt — a clear sign of credit stress. Warren Buffett also warning of fragility in the banking system from increased connection to non-bank players (Bloomberg, March 31). Pattern p018 (Regional Banking Crisis 2023) analog: credit stress triggers financials volatility and safe-haven flows into Treasuries (treasury_bid). financials_volatility confirmed via Buffett warnings + credit market stress signals. treasury_bid confirmed by US Bonds Rally on March 31 (Bloomberg Markets: 'US Bonds Rally as Traders Bet on Fed Rate Cut in About-Face') — flight to safety amid financial sector stress.

evidence_pack.summary: Private credit stress confirmed via Reuters + Buffett warnings. financials_volatility + treasury_bid confirmed. Regional Banking Crisis analog validated.
drivers: Private credit concessions; Buffett bank fragility warning; US bond rally (safe haven)

signal.title: Effective fed funds rate: 3.64% March 30th vs 3.64% March 27th
signal.reasoning: moderate source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.pair_trade_rationale: Rates and fiscal repricing usually favor self-funded quality over financing-dependent growth exposures.
signal.value_chain_notes: This is a macro/rates regime signal and should only map into sector chains when a more specific bottleneck is visible.

notes: Effective Fed Funds Rate: 3.64% March 30 vs 3.64% March 27 — unchanged. The signal predicted a front_end_yield_move and usd_trend_shift, but the Fed held steady at 3.64% across the three-day window. While the Fed is clearly watching private credit correction (Powell confirming correction on March 30), the actual rate environment remained unchanged. The signal's front_end_yield_move prediction was incorrect — no move observed. USD trend shift was also not driven by Fed action but by other factors (FX dynamics from Iran war). INCORRECT on the primary predicted outcome.

signal.title: Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war
signal.reasoning: high-credibility source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: Similar to Oct 2023 when Iran attacked Israel — oil spiked 5% intraday before settling. Defense stocks rallied on escalation risk while airlines dropped on fuel cost concerns. Watch for further retaliation.
signal.pair_trade_rationale: Defense electronics, EW, sensors, and avionics specialists with embedded program positions should outperform less differentiated downstream exposure.
signal.value_chain_notes: Sensors, avionics, tactical radios, EW, and targeting systems are hidden defense enablers with strong switching costs.

notes: FT confirmed (March 31): 'Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war' — international official holdings at New York Federal Reserve fell to lowest level since 2012. UnusualWhales also reported: 'Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war.' This directly confirms the signal's predicted outcomes. rates_volatility: Treasury market structurally unstable, requiring occasional official support (Bloomberg, March 31). FX_move: Dollar dynamics shifting as foreign official selling reduces bid for Treasuries. CORRECT on both primary predicted outcomes.
verification_note: FT confirmed (March 31): 'Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war' — international official holdings at New York Federal Reserve fell to lowest level since 2012. This directly confirms the signal's predicted outcomes. rates_volatility: Treasury market structurally unstable, requiring occasional official support (Bloomberg, March 31). evidence_pack.summary: Foreign central bank Treasury selling confirmed by FT. International Fed holdings at lowest since 2012. rates_volatility confirmed. fx_move confirmed via USD dynamics from reduced foreign official bid.
drivers: Foreign CB selling Treasuries; NY Fed holdings at 2012 low; Iran war risk
sector_impact: US Treasuries (under pressure); USD (weakening from reduced foreign bid); rates volatility
